log.context:
Good. The check `!user.username` correctly evaluates to true when username is null, which will trigger the auth prompt and hide content.

---

**Summary of findings and fixes:**

**Root cause:** `/api/me` returns HTTP 200 with `{"username": null}` for unauthenticated requests (not 401/403). All four protected pages only checked `r.status === 401 || r.status === 403`, which never triggered. The subsequent `if (!user)` check also passed because `{username: null}` is a truthy object in JavaScript.
